sourceMovie.title: The Net
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Net (1995) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Irwin Winkler's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hackers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Net clicked for you, Hackers (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Net?
faq.answer: Try Hackers, Untraceable, The Way of the Gun—each shares DNA with The Net while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Net?
faq.answer: The Net (1995) is best described as Crime, Drama, Mystery, Thriller, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Net part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Net Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
